deep_dive.title: Design principles for stable and generalizable data-driven discretizations for solving linear hyperbolic conservation laws
deep_dive.abstract: We investigate data-driven finite-volume discretizations of the linear advection equation in one dimension. Neural networks for use as numerical advection schemes are constructed adhering to first principles of numerical analysis, allowing us to examine how normalization, training data, and architectural choices influence stability, accuracy, and shape preservation. (i) We show that reconstruction based solely on cell averages leads to a multi-valued learning problem, explaining limited generalization when training data includes widely different curvature regimes. (ii) Numerical stability and good generalization can be achieved by enforcing semilinearity (Lin and Rood 1998) through local stencil-scale normalization, which ensures invariance under affine transformations of the inputs. (iii) A new data-driven flux limiter is introduced that outperforms the classical 'OSTVD3' (Arora and Roe, 1997) scheme in shape preservation by introducing mild antidiffusion in near-linear regimes, while higher-order reconstruction in non-monotonic regions provides limited benefit. (iv) We show that training on polynomial profiles yields stable, high-order accurate discretizations, with the polynomial degree controlling the formal order of accuracy. Together, these results illustrate how the representational, architectural, and training choices govern the stability and generalization of data-driven finite-volume schemes for linear advection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Data-driven discretization for advection equation, physics-informed principles for atmospheric transport
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 数据驱动平流方程离散化，物理信息原则用于大气传输

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Investigates data-driven finite-volume discretizations for linear advection, enforcing semilinearity for stability and generalization, directly relevant to physics-informed neural operators for atmospheric transport.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 线性平流方程的数据驱动离散化面临稳定性和泛化挑战。本文从数值分析第一原理出发，构建神经网络作为数值平流格式，发现仅基于单元平均重建会导致多值学习问题；通过强制半线性（Lin and Rood 1998）可实现稳定与良好泛化。方法可扩展至大气传输模型中的物理信息神经算子，为空气质量模拟提供保物理的机器学习替代方案。

quick_skim.title: Advances in Scientific Machine Learning for Coupled Fluid Flow and Transport
quick_skim.abstract: This chapter reviews recent advances in Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) for modeling coupled fluid flow and transport phenomena governed by the incompressible Navier-Stokes and scalar transport equations. Such systems, found in applications like turbidity currents and thermal convection, feature strong nonlinear coupling and multiscale behavior that make high-fidelity simulations computationally expensive. To address this, the chapter surveys state-of-the-art SciML methods for building efficient surrogate models, including linear reduced-order techniques based on Singular Value Decomposition (such as Dynamic Mode Decomposition) and nonlinear neural network approaches like Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) and $β$-Variational Autoencoders ($β$-VAEs). It first covers the authors' work combining these models with High Performance Computing strategies, including Adaptive Mesh Refinement/Coarsening (AMR/C) and scientific floating-point data compression. It then presents two new contributions: surrogate modeling of turbidity currents via PINNs, and the extraction of disentangled nonlinear modes from thermal flows using $β$-VAEs. Governing equations and representative benchmarks, including lock-exchange flows and Rayleigh-Bénard convection, illustrate these methodologies. The chapter is intentionally long, covering both the mathematical and physical foundations of coupled fluid flow and the computational aspects of state-of-the-art modeling. Overall, it demonstrates how SciML enables fast, accurate approximations of complex coupled systems within the specific data regimes and modeling assumptions considered, while substantially reducing computational cost relative to full-order simulations. Broader capabilities such as real-time prediction and uncertainty quantification remain active research directions whose feasibility depends strongly on the problem at hand.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: review of SciML for coupled fluid flow and transport, including PINNs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 科学机器学习在耦合流体流动与传输中的综述，包含物理信息神经网络
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 科学机器学习在耦合流体流动与传输中的综述，包含物理信息神经网络

quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该章综述了科学机器学习在耦合流体流动与传输（如浊流、热对流）建模中的最新进展。针对高保真仿真计算昂贵的问题，主要介绍了两类方法：基于奇异值分解的线性降阶技术和基于PINNs、β-VAE的非线性神经网络方法。这些方法为构建高效代理模型提供了系统工具，适用于环境流体等复杂系统。

quick_skim.title: Deep Spectral Learning of Embedded Latent Transfer Operators for Stochastic Dynamical Systems
quick_skim.abstract: We propose a spectral learning method for stochastic nonlinear dynamical systems represented with embedded latent transfer operators in deep feature spaces. We instantiate the method as Deep Spectral Encoder (DSE), an operator-based latent state-space model in which a time-invariant neural encoder implements learnable nonlinear feature maps from observations, and these features define Markovian latent states whose temporal evolution and observation mapping are described by the transfer and observation operators, respectively. Functional canonical correlation analysis in a learnable Galerkin-projected feature space provides state coordinates from past and future observations, and the two linear operators are estimated on the state coordinates as ridge-regularized closed-form solutions that coincide with Galerkin projections of the associated covariance operators. On this representation, we generalize sequential Bayesian filtering and Koopman spectral mode decomposition in feature space. Experiments on several scenarios show stable and superior performance with sequential Bayesian filtering and dynamic mode decomposition baselines even under noise and partial observability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: spectral learning of latent operators for dynamical systems, related to neural operator methods
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 潜变量传递算子的谱学习，与神经算子方法相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 潜变量传递算子的谱学习，与神经算子方法相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 潜变量传递算子的谱学习，与神经算子方法相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Deep Spectral Encoder for learning transfer operators in stochastic dynamical systems, relevant to neural operator surrogate models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对随机动力系统的潜变量传递算子谱学习问题，提出Deep Spectral Encoder方法，通过时间不变神经网络编码器学习非线性特征映射并构建马尔可夫潜状态，在Galerkin投影特征空间中利用泛函典型相关分析估计传递算子与观测算子。该方法为神经算子代理模型提供了理论基础，可迁移至空气质量模型加速。

quick_skim.title: A fast direct solver based neural network for solving PDEs
quick_skim.abstract: The matrices arising from large scale $N$-body problems can be efficiently represented using hierarchical matrices, whose key idea is that the admissible off-diagonal sub-matrices can be well approximated by low-rank matrices across a hierarchy of matrix partitions. HODLR (Hierarchical Off-Diagonal Low-Rank) matrices are a subclass of hierarchical matrices in which all off-diagonal submatrices at every level of a recursive binary partition are low-rank. In this article, we present a neural network that learns the inverse operation of HODLR matrices based on the fast direct solver for HODLR matrices developed by Ambikasaran and Darve (2013). We further extend the architecture to learn nonlinear solution operators associated with PDEs by replacing some of the linear layers with deep sub-networks. We demonstrate the performance of the proposed architecture by performing a comprehensive set of experiments that include (i) solving a linear problem such as the Fredholm integral equation of the second kind, (ii) solving PDEs such as the nonlinear Schrödinger equation, Burgers' equation, and the steady-state Darcy's flow equation, (iii) generalization study across varying parameter values, (iv) comparing the inference time of the proposed network with the run time of a classical numerical solver, and (v) comparing the proposed network with some of the existing neural operator learning networks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: neural network learns PDE solution operator using HODLR matrix, applicable to surrogate modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 神经网络利用HODLR矩阵学习PDE解算子，可应用于代理建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 神经网络利用HODLR矩阵学习PDE解算子，可应用于代理建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 神经网络利用HODLR矩阵学习PDE解算子，可应用于代理建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A neural network that learns the inverse of HODLR matrices and extends to nonlinear PDE solution operators, offering efficient surrogates.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对大规模偏微分方程求解的计算瓶颈，提出一种基于层级低秩矩阵快速直接求解器的神经网络。网络首先学习HODLR矩阵的逆运算，进而扩展为非线性PDE解算子。该方法为构建高效代理模型提供了新途径，尤其适用于涉及大规模N体问题的科学计算领域。

quick_skim.title: Conformal Candidate Certification for Offline Model-Based Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Offline model-based optimization (MBO) proposes candidates by optimizing a surrogate trained on a fixed historical dataset. Because candidates are deliberately out-of-distribution, surrogate rankings are least reliable exactly where the optimizer is most aggressive, yet existing methods provide no per-candidate statistical certificate that a design meets a target threshold. We propose \emph{Conformal Candidate Certification} (CCC), a post-hoc wrapper that attaches a calibrated one-sided lower bound to each candidate and advances only those whose bound exceeds the target. We show that entropy-regularized surrogate maximization induces a Gibbs-tilted proposal, so the same surrogate supplies importance weights for weighted conformal prediction without a separate density-ratio estimation step. In a controlled synthetic study, CCC certifies $16.7\%$ of an aggressive proposal pool with empirical coverage 0.990 at nominal 0.90, while standard conformal prediction ignoring the covariate shift collapses to 0.416 coverage.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Offline model-based optimization with surrogate model and conformal certification, applicable to air quality surrogate modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于替代模型的离线优化与保形认证，可应用于空气质量替代建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于替代模型的离线优化与保形认证，可应用于空气质量替代建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于替代模型的离线优化与保形认证，可应用于空气质量替代建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Conformal Candidate Certification (CCC) for offline surrogate-based optimization, providing calibrated lower bounds to certify candidates, directly applicable to air quality surrogate models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 离线模型优化中，替代模型在分布外候选上排名不可靠。本文提出保形候选认证（CCC），作为后处理包装器，为每个候选提供校准的单侧下限，仅推进超过阈值的候选。该方法利用熵正则化替代最大化产生吉布斯倾斜提议，实现加权保形预测，无需额外训练。实验表明CCC能有效筛选可靠设计，可迁移至空气质量替代模型优化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 离线模型优化中，替代模型在分布外候选上排名不可靠。本文提出保形候选认证（CCC），作为后处理包装器，为每个候选提供校准的单侧下限，仅推进超过阈值的候选。该方法利用熵正则化替代最大化产生吉布斯倾斜提议，实现加权保形预测，无需额外训练。实验表明CCC能有效筛选可靠设计，可迁移至空气质量替代模型优化。

quick_skim.title: RepNet: Tackling spectral bias in deep neural networks via parameter reparameterization
quick_skim.abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved remarkable success in scientific computing, yet they often suffer from spectral bias in capturing oscillatory and multiscale behaviors. In this study, we investigate this limitation by examining the failure of shallow ReLU neural networks in fitting high-frequency functions. This observation identifies two important factors in resolving rapid oscillations: the initial slope scale and the distribution of partition points induced by the networks. Motivated by this analysis, we propose RepNet, a reparameterized DNN model for ReLU and tanh networks designed for high-frequency and multiscale problems. The key idea is to reparameterize the weights and biases in the first hidden layer, which enables effective control of the initial slope scale and provides an appropriate distribution of the initial partition points. Furthermore, treating the reparameterized weights and biases as trainable parameters allows the DNN to achieve adaptive frequency scaling during training. In addition, we derive quantitative estimates for the output and slope magnitudes of the reparameterized DNN to guide the initialization of the proposed method. Numerical experiments, including multiscale one- and four-dimensional function approximation, forward and inverse PDE problems in combination with physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), and operator learning, demonstrate that RepNet improves the predicted accuracy of vanilla DNNs in capturing highly oscillatory features with slightly additional computational cost. These results indicate that RepNet provides an effective and flexible approach for overcoming spectral bias and applying DNNs to multiscale problems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reparameterization method to overcome spectral bias, enhancing neural network for multiscale environmental modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 参数重新参数化方法克服频谱偏差，增强神经网络在多尺度环境建模中的表现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 参数重新参数化方法克服频谱偏差，增强神经网络在多尺度环境建模中的表现

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RepNet reparameterizes first-layer weights and biases to enable high-frequency learning, mitigating spectral bias in scientific computing.

quick_skim.title: A Hybrid LSTM--Vision Transformer Architecture for Predicting HRRR Forecast Errors
quick_skim.abstract: Forecast errors in high-resolution numerical weather prediction (NWP) systems are often linked to unresolved planetary boundary layer (PBL) processes, convection, terrain-induced circulations, and other vertically structured atmospheric phenomena. Previous work demonstrated that Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks can successfully predict forecast errors in the High-Resolution Rapid Refresh (HRRR) model using mesonet observations, but we believe performance degradation is linked to periods of complex vertical atmospheric evolution. To address this limitation, we develop a hybrid LSTM-Vision Transformer (LSTM-ViT) framework that combines temporal sequence learning from surface observations with atmospheric profiles from the New York State Mesonet profiler network. The LSTM-ViT framework is trained to predict HRRR hourly precipitation, 10 m wind speed, and 2 m temperature forecast errors at individual mesonet stations. Across all three predictors, incorporation of profiler-derived atmospheric structure improves forecast error prediction skill relative to the baseline LSTM architecture, with the largest gains occurring at shorter forecast lead times and during periods of enhanced PBL activity. Improvements are particularly pronounced for precipitation forecast error, where the LSTM-ViT framework achieves approximately a twofold increase in predictive skill relative to the baseline LSTM while better capturing convectively driven error evolution and reducing degradation associated with PBL processes. These results demonstrate that combining temporal sequence learning with vertically informed attention mechanisms provides a physically meaningful pathway for improving forecast error prediction in operational NWP systems. Our research offers forecasters enhanced guidance regarding model bias and forecast confidence.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: deep learning for forecast error prediction, applicable to bias correction in air quality models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 深度学习预测预报误差，适用于空气质量模型的偏差校正
